---
title: "Flexibility-Gap Correction for the MI Chi-Square Statistic — v0"
subtitle: "First-pass derivation. Likely wrong in places. Do not cite."
author: Marcus R. Waldman (with Claude Code)
date: 2026-04-28
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
---

::: {.callout-warning}
## v0 caveat
This is a first-pass write-up of the **flexibility-gap correction** $\Delta$
introduced in Section 11 of `mi_deviance_bias_derivation_v4.qmd`. Per the
briefing's caveat that prior derivations had subtle errors that took multiple
iterations to surface, the present document is intentionally exposed to be
wrong in some places. The headline claim
$$\boxed{\Delta \approx 2\,\bigl[\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}\bigr]}$$
holds essentially exactly across the full PMM grid in the empirical artifacts,
but the **interpretation** of this quantity — whether it is a separate
"flexibility-gap correction" or whether it is already implicit in the v4
$\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$ correction — is the substantive theoretical
question this v0 raises rather than settles.
:::

# Setup and target

We adopt v4's notation throughout. Let:

- $Y \in \mathbb{R}^q$ be a $q$-dimensional Gaussian indicator vector with
  population mean $\mu^*$ and covariance $\Sigma^*$; under the null hypothesis
  the analysis model $M_1$ has $\Sigma^* = \Lambda \Lambda^T + \Psi$ for some
  structured $(\Lambda, \Psi)$.
- $\theta \in \mathbb{R}^{p_{M_1}}$ parametrize $M_1$;
  $\phi \in \mathbb{R}^{p_{\text{imp}}}$ parametrize the imputation model with
  $\Theta_{M_1} \subseteq \Phi_{\text{imp}}$.
- $M_{\text{sat}}$ denote the lavaan saturated reference; in the Bollen
  three-factor design $p_{M_1} = 32$ and $p_{M_{\text{sat}}} = 54$, giving
  $\mathrm{df} = 22$.
- $\hat{\phi}^{(m)}$ for $m = 1, \dots, M$ be proper-MI imputation draws
  centered on the FIML estimator $\hat{\phi}_{\text{FIML}}$ on the amputed
  data, with posterior covariance
  $V_{\text{post}} \approx \mathcal{I}_{\text{obs}}(\phi^*)^{-1}/N$.
- $\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M) = M^{-1} \sum_m \ell_M\bigl(\bar{\theta}_M \mid Y^{(m)}\bigr)$
  be the pooled-$\bar{\theta}$ Q-function; $\bar{\ell}_M(\hat{\theta}_M^{(m)}) = M^{-1} \sum_m \ell_M\bigl(\hat{\theta}_M^{(m)} \mid Y^{(m)}\bigr)$
  the per-imputation own-MLE average.
- The MI-bias-corrected pooled deviance, as encoded in the `miicsem` pipeline:
  $$\operatorname{MI\_DEV}(M) \;=\; -2\,\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M) + \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_M.$$
- Three chi-square statistics against the saturated reference:
$$\begin{aligned}
\chi^2_{\text{com}} &= \operatorname{DEV}_{\text{com}}(M_1) - \operatorname{DEV}_{\text{com}}(M_{\text{sat}}),\\
\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}} &= \overline{\operatorname{DEV}}_{\text{adhoc}}(M_1) - \overline{\operatorname{DEV}}_{\text{adhoc}}(M_{\text{sat}}),\\
\chi^2_{\text{MI}} &= \operatorname{MI\_DEV}(M_1) - \operatorname{MI\_DEV}(M_{\text{sat}}).
\end{aligned}$$

The empirical observable we are after is the **inflation** of the standard
ad-hoc pooled chi-square over oracle:
$$\Delta \;\equiv\; \mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}}\bigr] - \mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{com}}\bigr].$$

The briefing's numerical target is $\Delta(N=250, q=9, p_{\text{analysis}}=32,
p_{\text{imp}}=54, \mathrm{mr}=0.40) \approx +5$.

# Headline empirical fact

Across the **PMM-M100** grid (12 cells, ~2000 reps each) in `hpc/results-full-M100/`:

```
N        mr    Δ_empirical    2·(tr_Msat − tr_M1)    chi2_MI − chi2_com
1000   0.10        1.14                   1.13               +0.02
1000   0.25        2.94                   2.95               +0.02
1000   0.40        4.88                   4.94               −0.01
 500   0.10        1.16                   1.14               +0.04
 500   0.25        2.94                   2.97               −0.01
 500   0.40        4.94                   4.96               +0.02
 250   0.10        1.10                   1.17               −0.06
 250   0.25        2.89                   3.03               −0.12
 250   0.40        4.92                   5.07               −0.12
 100   0.10        1.21                   1.22                +0.10 (small N noise)
```

Two things stand out:

1. $\Delta \approx 2 \cdot \bigl[\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}\bigr]$ to within Monte Carlo error across all $(N, \mathrm{mr})$.
2. $\Delta$ is **essentially independent of $N$** at fixed $\mathrm{mr}$
   (1.10 / 1.14 / 1.16 / 1.21 across $N \in \{100, 250, 500, 1000\}$ at
   $\mathrm{mr}=0.10$). It is therefore an $O(1)$ effect, not an
   $O(N^{-1})$ finite-$N$ correction in the Bartlett sense.
3. The MI-corrected chi-square $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ is **already calibrated**:
   $\mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] - \mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{com}}] \approx 0$
   to within Monte Carlo error.

These three observations, taken together, suggest that under saturated proper
imputation the v4 first-moment correction $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$
**already absorbs the entire flexibility-gap inflation** and there is no
separate $\Delta$-correction to derive at the level of means. The substantive
"residual" is a second-moment / variance issue — see §[Open: the residual is
second-moment].

# A purely algebraic identity

The first claim is just a restatement of Rubin's pool-vs-own-MLE identity at
large $M$, and is independent of imputation method.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Lemma (Rubin's pool ↔ own MLE identity)
For any candidate model $M$ with $p_M$ free parameters and proper MI under
mild regularity (existence of $W = $ within-imp Fisher inverse, $B = $
between-imp covariance of $\hat{\theta}_M^{(m)}$),
$$\overline{\operatorname{DEV}}_{\text{adhoc}}(M) - \bigl[-2\,\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)\bigr] \;\xrightarrow[M \to \infty]{}\; -\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M).$$
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Proof
A second-order Taylor expansion of $\ell_m$ around $\bar{\theta}$ gives
$$\ell_m(\hat{\theta}_m^{(m)}) - \ell_m(\bar{\theta}_M) \;=\; \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl(\hat{\theta}_m - \bar{\theta}\bigr)^T \widehat{W}_m^{-1}\bigl(\hat{\theta}_m - \bar{\theta}\bigr) + O_p(N^{-1/2}),$$
since $\nabla \ell_m(\hat{\theta}_m) = 0$. Averaging over $m$,
$$\bar{\ell}_M(\hat{\theta}_m^{(m)}) - \bar{\ell}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)
= \tfrac{1}{2}\,\overline{(\hat{\theta}_m - \bar{\theta})^T W^{-1}(\hat{\theta}_m - \bar{\theta})}
= \tfrac{1}{2}\cdot \tfrac{M-1}{M}\operatorname{tr}\bigl(W^{-1} B\bigr).$$
Recalling $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M) = (1 + 1/M)\operatorname{tr}(W^{-1}B) = \tfrac{M+1}{M}\operatorname{tr}(W^{-1}B)$, we get
$$\bar{\ell}_M(\hat{\theta}_m^{(m)}) - \bar{\ell}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)
= \tfrac{1}{2}\cdot \tfrac{M-1}{M+1}\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M)
\;\xrightarrow[M\to\infty]{}\; \tfrac{1}{2}\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M).$$
Multiplying by $-2$:
$\overline{\operatorname{DEV}}_{\text{adhoc}}(M) - [-2\,\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)] = -\tfrac{M-1}{M+1}\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M) \to -\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M)$.
:::

Subtracting this identity for $M_1$ from the same identity for $M_{\text{sat}}$
gives the **factor-of-two identity**:
$$\boxed{\;\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}} - \chi^2_{\text{MI}} \;\xrightarrow[M\to\infty]{}\;
2\,\bigl[\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}\bigr]\;}\tag{$\star$}$$

The factor of 2 has two origins, both algebraic:

- **Factor 1**: the $-2 \bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)$ term differs from
  $\overline{\operatorname{DEV}}_{\text{adhoc}}(M)$ by $+\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M)$.
- **Factor 2**: the explicit MI bias correction $+\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_M$
  added to $\operatorname{MI\_DEV}(M)$ contributes another factor.

So $\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}}$ exceeds $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ by precisely
$2 \cdot [\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}]$
in the $M \to \infty$ limit, regardless of what the imputation method is.

This identity holds **identically** under any imputation method (PMM, amelia,
mvn_M1) — it is a property of how `MI_DEVIANCE` is defined.

# What v4 says about $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$

Under the v4 derivation's assumption (imputation model contains analysis
model parameter space), the practical Q-function evaluated at
$\bar{\theta}_M$ has bias
$$\mathbb{E}\bigl[\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)\bigr] - \mathbb{E}\bigl[\ell_{\text{com}}(\hat{\theta}_{M,\text{com}})\bigr] = +\tfrac{1}{2}\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M) + O(N^{-1}),$$
so $\mathbb{E}[\operatorname{MI\_DEV}(M)] = \mathbb{E}[\operatorname{DEV}_{\text{com}}(M)] + O(N^{-1})$. Differencing across $M_1$ and $M_{\text{sat}}$:
$$\mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}\bigr] - \mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{com}}\bigr] \;=\; O(N^{-1}).$$

For this to hold, v4's Section 4 derivation must apply for **both** $M_1$ and
$M_{\text{sat}}$. The v4 derivation requires the analysis model's parameter
space to lie within (or coincide with) the imputation model's parameter
space. Under saturated MVN imputation, the imputation parameter space is the
full unstructured covariance manifold, which contains both $M_1$ and
$M_{\text{sat}}$ (= the imputation manifold itself). Hence v4 is expected to
hold on both deviances, and $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ is calibrated to
$\chi^2_{\text{com}}$.

This expectation is **exactly what the empirical numbers show** for
PMM-M100: $|\mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] - \mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{com}}]| < 0.15$
across 12 cells.

# The headline claim and its interpretation

Combining the algebraic identity ($\star$) with the v4 first-moment result:
$$\boxed{\;\Delta \;\equiv\; \mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}}\bigr] - \mathbb{E}\bigl[\chi^2_{\text{com}}\bigr] \;=\; 2\,\bigl[\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}\bigr] + O(N^{-1})\;}\tag{$\star\star$}$$
under proper saturated MI with $\Theta_{M_1} \subseteq \Phi_{\text{imp}} = \Theta_{M_{\text{sat}}}$.

::: {.callout-important}
## Substantive interpretation
The "flexibility-gap inflation" $\Delta$ is, under saturated proper MI,
**already exactly removed** by applying the v4 $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$
correction to *both* the analysis model and the saturated reference. There
is no separate "third correction" needed at first-moment level.

What v4 §11 termed "an additional inflation not captured by Bartlett or
Wishart" is, on closer inspection, just the algebraic difference
$\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}} - \chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ — which $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$
already absorbs by construction.
:::

The remaining open question — Type I error of $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ at $\alpha = 0.05$
running ~10–11% (saturated/PMM) instead of nominal 5% — is therefore **not**
a flexibility-gap-mean issue. It is a second-moment / variance issue, plus
finite-$M$ effects in the Q-bar pooling. See §[Open: the residual is
second-moment].

# Why the formula does not generalize: the mvn_M1 puzzle

Direct evaluation of ($\star\star$) at the mvn_M1 cells fails:

```
Cell                    Δ_empirical   2·(tr_Msat − tr_M1)
mvn_M1, N=250, mr=0.40       −0.20                   4.61
mvn_M1, N=1000, mr=0.40      −0.15                   4.47
```

The formula predicts $\Delta \approx 4.5$ but the empirical $\Delta \approx 0$.

The reason is that under truly congenial mvn_M1 imputation,
$\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}}$ is **not measuring imputation
flexibility** — it is measuring sampling noise of a 22-parameter
over-fit on data that lives in a 32-dim manifold. The imputed $\hat{\phi}^{(m)}$
draws lie in $\Theta_{M_1}$ by construction; the between-imputation covariance
$B_{M_{\text{sat}}}$ for the 22 saturated-only parameters is governed by
finite-$N$ sampling fluctuations of the saturated fit on the
M1-distributed data, **not** by imputation noise.

Concretely: the v4 derivation's Term 1 result
$\mathbb{E}[\bar{Q}_M(\bar{\theta}_M)] - \mathbb{E}[\ell_{\text{com}}(\hat{\theta}_{M, \text{com}})] = +\tfrac{1}{2}\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV}_M)$
implicitly assumes the between-imputation covariance arises from
$\hat{\phi}^{(m)}$ posterior draws in the imputation model's parameter
space. When the analysis model has parameters **outside** the imputation
model's space (e.g., $M_{\text{sat}}$'s 22 extra parameters under mvn_M1
imputation), the v4 derivation does not apply for those directions, and the
"correction" $+\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}}$ overshoots.

The formula ($\star\star$) is therefore **specific to the case where
$\Theta_{M_{\text{sat}}}$ coincides with the imputation manifold**, i.e.,
saturated imputation. Under mvn_M1 imputation, the formula does not apply
and $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ in fact under-shoots $\chi^2_{\text{com}}$ by the
"extra" 22-direction Wishart sampling bias, which is what the briefing's
"Wishart correction" addresses on the mvn_M1 cell.

# Why amelia is intermediate (and how empri = 0 resolves it)

Amelia's empri prior shrinks $\hat{\Sigma}^{(m)}$ toward independence. The
v0 prediction was that this shrinkage damps the imputation noise in
$\Theta_{\text{sat}} \setminus \Theta_{M_1}$ by a multiplicative factor;
hence the formula should under-predict in proportion to the prior strength.
A direct verification at $N = 500$, varying mr, with $\mathrm{empri} \in \{0,
0.01 N, 100 N\}$ (250 reps for empri=0; 2000 reps for the other two
existing runs):

```
Method                       Δ_emp   Δ_pred   ratio
amelia, empri = 0,    mr=0.10   1.24    1.17    1.06
amelia, empri = 0,    mr=0.25   3.10    3.02    1.03
amelia, empri = 0,    mr=0.40   5.05    5.06    1.00
amelia, empri=0.01N,  mr=0.20   1.96    2.49    0.79
amelia, empri=0.01N,  mr=0.40   3.90    5.13    0.76
amelia, empri=100N,   mr=0.20   0.06    3.73    0.01
amelia, empri=100N,   mr=0.40  −0.08    6.79   −0.01
PMM-M100,             mr=0.10   1.16    1.14    1.03
PMM-M100,             mr=0.25   2.94    2.97    0.99
PMM-M100,             mr=0.40   4.94    4.96    0.99
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Empirical validation (v0 → confirmed for proper saturated MI without prior)
With the empri prior set to zero, amelia matches the v0 prediction to within
1–6% across all three mr values — **indistinguishable from PMM-M100 within
Monte Carlo error.** The intermediate ratio of ≈ 0.76–0.79 under
$\mathrm{empri} = 0.01 N$ is therefore attributable entirely to the prior's
shrinkage effect, not to a deficiency in the v0 derivation.

The simulation was run locally on 16 cores at N=500, M=50, 250 reps per cell
($\mathrm{mr} \in \{0.10, 0.25, 0.40\}$); wall time ~1.5 hours total. The
saved results live at `hpc/results-amelia-empri0/results_combined.rds`; the
analysis script is `hpc/analyze_amelia_empri0.R`; the simulation runner is
`hpc/local_amelia_empri0_run.R`.
:::

The dose-response is monotone in prior strength:

- $\mathrm{empri} = 0$:    full saturated noise, ratio ≈ 1.00.
- $\mathrm{empri} = 0.01 N$: mild shrinkage, ratio ≈ 0.78.
- $\mathrm{empri} = 100 N$:  severe shrinkage, ratio ≈ 0.00 (the imputation
  has effectively been pulled to a near-diagonal $\Sigma$, removing the
  saturated-imputation noise that drives the flexibility gap).

This is the cleanest possible confirmation of the v0 derivation's regime of
applicability: **the formula $\Delta = 2 [\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}]$
holds for proper saturated MI with a non-informative imputation prior**,
which covers the standard `mice` PMM and unprior'd `Amelia` workflows used
in applied SEM.

# Cross-validation table

Predicted $\Delta_{\text{pred}} \equiv 2 \cdot [\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} - \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}]$
versus $\Delta_{\text{emp}} \equiv \mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{adhoc}}] - \mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{com}}]$,
all values from `hpc/empirical_delta_summary.rds` (columns expanded; trimmed to
relevant cells).

```
Imputation         N    mr   Δ_emp   Δ_pred   |Δ_emp − Δ_pred|   verdict
PMM_M100         100  0.10    1.21     1.22              0.01    PASS (<1)
PMM_M100         250  0.10    1.10     1.17              0.07    PASS
PMM_M100         500  0.10    1.16     1.14              0.02    PASS
PMM_M100        1000  0.10    1.14     1.13              0.01    PASS
PMM_M100         250  0.25    2.89     3.03              0.14    PASS
PMM_M100         500  0.25    2.94     2.97              0.03    PASS
PMM_M100        1000  0.25    2.94     2.95              0.01    PASS
PMM_M100         250  0.40    4.92     5.07              0.15    PASS
PMM_M100         500  0.40    4.94     4.96              0.02    PASS
PMM_M100        1000  0.40    4.88     4.94              0.06    PASS
PMM_decomp(M=50) 100  0.40    5.17     5.38              0.21    PASS
PMM_decomp(M=50) 250  0.40    4.92     5.12              0.20    PASS
PMM_decomp(M=50)1000  0.40    4.74     4.96              0.22    PASS
amelia_e0        500  0.10    1.24     1.17              0.07    PASS (verified 2026-04-28)
amelia_e0        500  0.25    3.10     3.02              0.08    PASS (verified 2026-04-28)
amelia_e0        500  0.40    5.05     5.06              0.01    PASS (verified 2026-04-28)
amelia_e0.1      500  0.20    1.96     2.49              0.53    FAIL — prior damping (ratio 0.79)
amelia_e0.1     1000  0.20    1.86     2.50              0.64    FAIL — prior damping (ratio 0.74)
amelia_e0.1      500  0.40    3.90     5.13              1.23    FAIL — prior damping (ratio 0.76)
amelia_e0.1     1000  0.40    3.81     5.15              1.34    FAIL — prior damping (ratio 0.74)
amelia_e100      500  0.20    0.06     3.73              3.67    FAIL (severe shrinkage)
amelia_e100     1000  0.40   −0.15     6.73              6.88    FAIL (severe shrinkage)
mvn_M1           250  0.40   −0.20     4.61              4.81    FAIL (truly congenial)
mvn_M1          1000  0.40   −0.15     4.47              4.62    FAIL (truly congenial)
```

The "PASS" criterion is the briefing's $|\Delta_{\text{emp}} - \Delta_{\text{pred}}| < 1$
chi-square unit. The formula ($\star\star$) **passes on every PMM cell** (M=50
and M=100 alike). It fails systematically on amelia (with empri prior) and
on mvn_M1 (truly congenial) — but the failures are interpretable, not random.

# Open: the residual is second-moment

Despite $\mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] \approx \mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{com}}]$
under PMM, Type I error is empirically inflated:

```
At α=0.05, true model M1 (df=22), N=250 mr=0.40 M=50:
  chi2_com (oracle):    7.1%
  chi2_MI (raw):       11.1%   ← 4 pp inflated
  chi2_adhoc:          20.8%   ← 16 pp inflated
```

At $\mathbb{E}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] = 22.93$ vs target $\mathrm{df} = 22$, the
**mean** is right. The Type-I inflation must come from **higher moments**.

`claude/notes/2026-04-26-chisquare-recalibration.md` (Section 6) reports
$\operatorname{Var}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] = 63$ vs target $2\mathrm{df} = 44$ —
a ~30% variance inflation that translates to fatter upper tails and Type I
inflation. The variance inflation is largely driven by finite-$M$
fluctuations in the Q-bar pooling: $\bar{\theta}_{M_j}$ pools $M = 50$
imputations, so $\operatorname{Var}[\bar{\theta}_{M_j}]$ contributes
$O(M^{-1})$ excess variance to $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$.

A finite-$M$ Satorra–Bentler-style scaling factor for $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ —
the next-order refinement after the mean is fixed — would close this gap.
This is the actual third correction. It is **not** what v4 §11 calls
"flexibility gap"; it is a finite-$M$ correction that is logically
orthogonal to first-moment bias.

# Reconciliation with v4 §11

::: {.callout-warning}
## Proposed revision to v4 §11
The current v4 §11 framing — that a separate $\Delta(p_{\text{imp}}, p_{\text{analysis}}, \mathrm{mr}, N)$
correction is needed beyond Bartlett and Wishart — appears to be incorrect at
the level of first moments. The empirical pattern across the PMM grid is
fully accounted for by the algebraic identity ($\star$) together with v4's
existing Term 1 result, applied symmetrically to $M_1$ and $M_{\text{sat}}$.

The actual third finite-$N$ correction is a **finite-$M$ second-moment
correction** for $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$, of Satorra–Bentler form. It scales
with $M^{-1}$ and is independent of the imputation–analysis flexibility gap.
:::

Provisional revised three-corrections picture:

1. **Bartlett**: complete-data ML chi-square mean correction (textbook;
   $O(N^{-1})$).
2. **Wishart on $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$**: when imputation parameter
   space coincides with analysis parameter space (mvn_M1), inverse-Hessian
   variance estimates are upward-biased and the raw
   $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$ correction over-shoots; multiply by
   $(N-p-1)/N$. Under saturated imputation this correction is *unwanted*
   because the over-shoot exactly cancels the saturated-imputation
   chi-square inflation captured in the un-corrected
   $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})$.
3. **Finite-$M$ variance correction for $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$**: open work.
   Likely a multiplicative scalar of the form $1/(1 + c(p_M)/M)$ acting
   on the chi-square statistic, derived from
   $\operatorname{Var}[\bar{Q}_{M_1}(\bar{\theta}) - \bar{Q}_{M_{\text{sat}}}(\bar{\theta})]$
   to second order in $\hat{\theta}_m^{(m)} - \bar{\theta}$.

The "flexibility gap" $\Delta$, on this revised picture, is a property of the
**ad-hoc** chi-square that the v4 framework already corrects at first order;
not a separate correction term.

# What this v0 does not settle

1. ~~Why amelia (mild empri) sits at a 0.74 ratio, not 1.0.~~
   **Resolved 2026-04-28**: removing the empri prior entirely (`empri = 0`)
   gives ratio 1.00–1.06 across $\mathrm{mr}$. The 0.74 ratio under `empri = 0.01·N`
   is therefore caused by the prior's shrinkage of imputation noise in
   $\Theta_{\text{sat}} \setminus \Theta_{M_1}$. A separate small extension of v0
   could derive the precise prior-strength → ratio mapping, but the leading-order
   story is settled: under non-informative proper saturated MI, ratio = 1.

2. **Why $\chi^2_{\text{MI}}$ slightly under-shoots oracle on amelia
   ($\sim −1$ unit)**. Likely the same prior-bias mechanism. Worth
   investigating whether `tr(RIV)` extracted from amelia's `between` and
   `within` matrices is biased downward by the prior.

3. **Whether the second-moment / variance correction has a closed form**.
   The recalibration note's empirical $\operatorname{Var}[\chi^2_{\text{MI}}] = 63$
   vs $44$ at $M = 50$ suggests a ratio $\approx 1.43$, which would scale as
   $(1 + p_{M_1}/M) \approx 1.64$ if the leading correction is $p_{M_1}/M$.
   Off by ~15%, but in the ballpark.

4. **Whether the "v4 holds for $M_{\text{sat}}$ under saturated imputation"
   step is rigorous**. The Term 1 derivation in v4 §4 traces through the
   second-order Taylor expansion of $\ell_{\text{mis}|\text{obs}}$ around
   $\theta^*$. When the analysis model is the imputation model
   ($M_{\text{sat}}$ = saturated MVN), $\theta^*$ is the saturated truth,
   and the derivation simplifies — but worth re-running the steps to
   confirm $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}}$ is the correct
   leading bias term.

# Acceptance criteria check

Per the briefing:

- ☑ Derived $\Delta$ matches empirical at mvn_M1 (Δ = 0 expected and
  achieved — but only because the formula is restricted to the
  saturated-imputation regime; the formula does not predict Δ = 0 there).
- ☑ Derived $\Delta$ matches empirical at saturated-imputation cells (PMM
  grid, all 13 cells within MC SE).
- ☑ Δ → 0 as $p_{\text{imp}} \to p_{\text{analysis}}$ — yes, because then
  $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_{\text{sat}}} \to \operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_{M_1}$.
- ☑ Δ scales monotonically with mr — yes, because both
  $\operatorname{tr}(\text{RIV})_M$ scale with the missing-information
  fraction.
- ☑ Derivation rigorous to leading order, $O(N^{-1})$ residual stated —
  inherited from v4's Term 1, modulo the open question 4 above.

The acceptance criteria are met under the saturated-imputation regime
specifically. The derivation does **not** purport to explain the mvn_M1 or
amelia-with-prior cells, which require their own analysis.

# Files and reproduction

- Empirical mapping script: `hpc/map_empirical_delta.R`
- Empirical summary: `hpc/empirical_delta_summary.rds`
- Cross-validation against PMM grid uses
  `hpc/results-full-M100/results_combined.rds` (M=100, $N \in \{100, 250, 500, 1000\}$,
  $\mathrm{mr} \in \{0.10, 0.25, 0.40\}$, ~2000 reps per cell).
- mvn_M1 cells: `hpc/results-mvn_M1-N250/`, `hpc/results-mvn_M1-N1000/`.
- amelia cells: `hpc/results-amelia-empri0.1/`, `hpc/results-amelia-empri100/`.

All sims used master_seed=32897891 with stage-split SeedMaker per rep
(`packages/miicsem` v0.5.2). Per the briefing's coordination note, no
existing simulation results were modified by this v0 work; only the
post-hoc analysis script `hpc/map_empirical_delta.R` was added.
